source.name: xai-org/x-algorithm
source.caveat: Use as public ranking-system evidence and a content pressure-test, not as a guarantee of private production weights.

retrieval: out-of-network candidates from ML retrieval over the global corpus
ranking_frame.ranking: Phoenix/Grok predicts engagement probabilities for candidate posts; a weighted scorer combines predicted actions into a final score.
ranking_frame.selection: Candidates are sorted by score and a top-K selector chooses the highest-ranked items.
ranking_frame.filtering: Pre- and post-scoring filters remove duplicates, already-seen items, self posts, muted or blocked contexts, ineligible posts, and final visibility failures.

pressure_tests: Does the opener create a concrete reason to reply, not just nod?
Would someone repost or quote this because it gives them a useful stance?
Does the post create enough curiosity for profile clicks without becoming clickbait?
Does the body reward dwell with a real idea, source, or operator move?
Does the framing avoid rage bait, vague AI hype, low-trust claims, or other negative feedback triggers?
Does the reply add enough context to rank as useful conversation instead of a low-effort reaction?
Does the content avoid repeating a served idea so it does not feel like duplicate feed material?
content_rules: Open with the operator problem, not the source title.
Make the practical move explicit enough that a reader can use it today.
Use sources to support the argument, not as the argument itself.
Prefer specific disagreement or decision framing over generic advice.
Write replies that add context, controls, or a sharper frame rather than empty agreement.
Do not manufacture engagement bait; optimize for useful responses and trust.
Avoid claims that depend on private X ranking weights; use this only as a public pressure-test lens.

signals: For You combines in-network and out-of-network retrieval.
Phoenix/Grok ranking predicts multiple engagement probabilities.
Filtering removes duplicates, old posts, self posts, blocked or muted contexts, and previously seen material.

The weighted score includes favorite, reply, repost, photo expand, click, profile click, video quality view, share, dwell, quote, continuous dwell time, follow author, and negative-feedback predictions.
Negative feedback terms include not interested, block author, mute author, and report.
Phoenix scorer requests model predictions for candidates using user scoring sequence context.
Ranking uses a home timeline product surface and model cluster selection.
Selection is score-based top-K after ranking.
Previously seen and bloom-filtered related post IDs are removed before serving.
Reply ranking uses a Grok/VLM scorer and structured score/reason output.
